name: Robert Tonyan
headline: Fading veteran tight end buried on Pittsburgh's chart
body:
Tonyan's 2025 was effectively a non-season: two games in Kansas City, an 11.1 percent snap share, 38 routes, and a single target that produced one catch for one yard and 0.1 fantasy points, alongside a brutal -0.519 EPA per play. That continues a steep slide from his 2023 peak in Chicago, when he played 12 games on a 28.2 percent snap share, ran 157 routes, and turned 17 targets into 11 catches for 112 yards and 11.2 points at TE66. At 32, the trend line points only one direction.

Now in Pittsburgh, he sits third on the tight-end depth chart behind blocking specialist Darnell Washington and pass-catcher Pat Freiermuth, capping any target path. His value is purely TD-dependent and matchup-driven, and even that requires injuries ahead of him. There is no redraft case here: he is a waiver-wire name, not a streaming option, and should be ignored in all standard leagues for 2026.
